summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting and public hearing on zoning case ZC2023-005, which involved text amendments to the township's zoning resolution. The amendments addressed several items including administrative relief authority for the zoning inspector, barn and storage structure uses, flag lot dimensions, accessory structure standards, and new definitions. The amendments had been recommended for approval by the Clermont County Planning Commission and approved by the Zoning Commission on August 1st. The Board of Trustees unanimously approved the text amendments as presented.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously (3-0)
Motion to approve zoning text amendments ZC2023-005 as presented passed unanimously (3-0)
notable_topics: Zoning text amendments to the zoning resolution (ZC2023-005)
Authorizing zoning inspector to grant minor administrative relief (Section 2.6a.5)
Adding use for barns and storage structures (Table 5.1, Section 5.4a.2)
Allowing accessory structures on adjacent lots under common ownership (Section 7.1b)
Standards for accessory structures in front yard in R1 district (Section 7.1c)
New definitions for agricultural building, agricultural, and barn storage structure (Section 16.3)
notes: No public comments were made during the hearing. The meeting was very brief, adjourning at approximately 6:06 PM. The Clermont County Planning Commission had recommended approval and the Zoning Commission approved the amendments on August 1st. The township itself was the applicant for these zoning text amendments.
title: BOT Special Meeting 8-8-23
